regions.eastern-caribbean: Eastern Caribbean weather for March 7, 2026, features unsettled conditions with thunderstorms and east winds 13 to 17 knots across the region. San Juan, St. Thomas, and St. Croix will experience periods of heavy rain and gusty winds, reducing visibility and making open water operations challenging. Mariners should secure vessels and avoid exposed anchorages, particularly in San Juan Harbor where thunderstorm activity is highest. Passengers are advised to plan indoor activities today and expect wet conditions through March 8, with improving but overcast skies by March 9.
regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean conditions for March 7, 2026, show stable weather with temperatures near 78 to 81 degrees and light east to east-northeast winds between 10 and 12 knots. Cozumel and nearby ports such as Costa Maya and Belize City can expect partly cloudy skies today with minimal rain risk, but isolated showers may develop late in the day, so secure loose gear and plan for brief wet conditions. Fishing captains targeting offshore waters around Roatan and Grand Cayman should note moderate easterly winds creating light chop; adjust anchorage positions to maintain shelter from easterly swell. Passengers should enjoy outdoor activities early and remain flexible for afternoon showers in coastal areas like Ocho Rios and Falmouth.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region weather for March 7, 2026: Nassau currently clear skies, 76F, east winds 8 knots, no rain expected. Conditions remain stable across Freeport, Bimini, Berry Islands, and Turks & Caicos with moderate east winds 8 to 15 knots over the next three days. Daytime temperatures will peak near 81F with light chances of rain, increasing slightly to 10 percent drizzle by day three. Mariners should maintain routine anchor checks in exposed areas like Bimini and Turks due to steady east winds; fishing captains can expect calm seas ideal for offshore trips today and tomorrow. Passengers advised to enjoy outdoor activities while monitoring cloud cover late in the period.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean conditions on March 7, 2026, show steady east winds at 19 knots around Aruba with temperatures near 79 degrees and partly cloudy skies. Expect similar wind speeds and overcast skies through the next three days with minimal rain chances, particularly low by day three. Mariners operating near Aruba, Curacao, Bonaire, and Cartagena should prepare for stable easterly trades, maintaining good sea states but watch for occasional reduced visibility under persistent cloud cover. Yacht and fishing captains should secure lighter gear and monitor wind shifts while cruise passengers are advised to plan outdoor activities with some overcast and occasional spray in mind.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean weather on March 7 shows mild conditions with temperatures near 79 to 80 degrees Fahrenheit and moderate east-northeast winds around 11 to 12 knots. Expect partly cloudy skies with a low chance of rain today at 21 percent, increasing slightly to scattered showers over the next two days, particularly near Grand Cayman and Belize City. Mariners should remain vigilant for brief rain squalls when navigating coastal waters or approaching Roatan and Cozumel harbors, as visibility may be reduced. Passengers should plan outdoor activities with some flexibility, and fishing captains should monitor wind shifts near Costa Maya to optimize anchorage and vessel stability.
regions.lesser-antilles: Expect breezy conditions across the Lesser Antilles today with east-northeast winds steady at 18 to 20 knots and temperatures in the low 80s. Barbados and nearby ports like St. Lucia and Martinique will see partly cloudy skies with a low chance of rain, though brief showers are possible later in the day, increasing to moderate levels by tomorrow. Mariners should secure loose gear and maintain vigilant watch for sudden squalls, especially when approaching exposed anchorages such as St. Vincent or Grenada. Passengers should prepare for occasional wet weather and moderate sea conditions when planning shore excursions.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles and surrounding ports are enjoying clear, calm conditions this morning with light NNE winds at 8 knots and temperatures near 57 degrees. Expect strengthening NE winds to 19 knots by afternoon with highs reaching 78 degrees and continued clear skies, ideal for docking or departure from Los Angeles, Long Beach, and Marina del Rey. Catalina Island will see increasing ESE winds at 17 knots and overcast skies tomorrow with temperatures climbing to 85 degrees, so secure light moorings and monitor wind shifts. Fishing captains should plan for easing SSE winds near 12 knots and partly cloudy skies by day three, with minimal rain chances throughout the period.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region reports clear skies with light WNW winds at 8 knots and a cool 53 degrees this morning. Expect a warming trend today with overcast skies and ENE winds increasing to 15 knots, minimal rain chance; good conditions for daytime operations at Ensenada and Punta Banda ports. Drizzle returns Tuesday and Wednesday with SW to S winds around 10 knots and a 41 percent rain chance, reducing visibility and requiring heightened caution near Islas Todos Santos anchorage. Mariners should secure loose gear ahead of the overcast shift and monitor local updates for changing wind directions affecting harbor approaches.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas region presents mild conditions today with temperatures near 71 degrees and light NNW winds at 9 knots. Skies will remain partly cloudy with no precipitation expected, offering good visibility for port operations at Cabo San Lucas and San Jose del Cabo. Over the next three days, expect increasing overcast skies with light to moderate NW and NNE winds between 7 and 13 knots, and minimal rain chances under 5 percent. Mariners should monitor wind shifts when maneuvering in La Paz Bay, and fishing captains can plan early morning trips before cloud cover thickens.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlan region reports steady overcast skies with light SSE winds at 5 knots and a low rain chance of 21 percent this morning. Expect similar conditions through the next three days with winds shifting from southwest to northwest between 9 and 10 knots and rain chances diminishing to under 10 percent by day three. Mariners should prepare for moderate seas and maintain cautious navigation near Mazatlan and Topolobampo ports due to reduced visibility. Fishing captains will find stable conditions for nearshore operations, but keep an eye on wind shifts affecting anchorage comfort in Altata Bay.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta region will experience mild conditions today with light northeast winds at 2 knots and partly cloudy skies, keeping seas calm for all ports including Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala. Over the next three days, expect steady overcast skies with increasing northwest winds between 8 and 10 knots, maintaining smooth but slightly choppier seas. Rain chances remain minimal, so fishing and yacht operations can proceed with confidence, though caution is advised when navigating nearshore areas in Punta Mita due to shifting wind directions. Cruise passengers should plan outdoor activities accordingly, as limited sun exposure will prevail but conditions remain stable.
regions.western-mediterranean: Western Mediterranean conditions for March 7, 2026 show mild temperatures near 57 Fahrenheit with light ESE winds around 6 knots and overcast skies in Barcelona. Expect deteriorating weather later today with east winds increasing to 10 knots and thunderstorms bringing 100 percent rain probability; caution advised when approaching Barcelona and Palma de Mallorca harbors due to reduced visibility and rougher seas. Valencia and Ibiza will also see unstable conditions; fishing activities should be limited and secure all loose gear. By day three, winds shift to WSW at 6 knots with fog developing near Malaga and Cadiz, requiring reduced speeds and use of radar for safe navigation.
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean conditions for March 7, 2026 show mild temperatures with light northeast to north-northeast winds averaging 2 to 5 knots. Naples and surrounding ports including Civitavecchia and Livorno will experience overcast skies with minimal rain chances, improving visibility but limited sun. Mariners should expect calm seas favorable for smooth navigation, while fishing captains can plan morning operations before any isolated showers arrive on day two. Passengers should dress in layers for cool, damp conditions and be prepared for occasional cloud cover throughout the week.
regions.eastern-mediterranean: Eastern Mediterranean weather on March 7, 2026, is stable with light northeast winds around 7-8 knots and overcast skies across key ports including Athens, Santorini, Mykonos, Rhodes, Corfu, Istanbul, Izmir, Cyprus, Haifa, and Alexandria. Temperatures remain mild, ranging from upper 50s to mid-60s Fahrenheit, with no precipitation expected through the next three days. Mariners should anticipate calm sea states and maintain routine navigation with minimal weather-related adjustments. Passengers can enjoy comfortable conditions for outdoor activities, while captains should note consistent NE winds favoring east-facing anchorages such as Piraeus and Izmir for secure mooring.
